返回本批次

EmDash：CMS 的全新呈现

EmDash: A Fresh Take on CMS

maciekpalmowski.dev 作者 taubek 分数 58 评论 14 2026/04/03 00:27
打开 HN 原帖 打开原始文章

中文摘要

  1. 今天是 4 月 1 日。由 Cloudflare 构建、Astro 提供支持的新 CMS 被定位为 WordPress 的现代精神继承者。当然。很搞笑。但这不是一个玩笑。 EmDash 是真实的，我当然要去看看。两篇文章让我开始：Cloudflare 自己的公告和 Joost 的看法。它是什么？ EmDash 是一个基于 TypeScript 和 Astro 6.0 构建的完全开源的 CMS。它在 Cloudflare Workers（或任何 Node.js 服务器）上无服务器运行，在本地使用 SQLite，在生产中使用 Cloudflare D1，并将内容存储为可移植文本（结构化 JSON）而不是原始 HTML。球场？如果您今天从头开始构建 WordPress，那么这就是 WordPress 的样子，而没有 20 年的遗留决策的拖累。我真正喜欢的东西 安全模型确实很聪明。在 WordPress 中，插件几乎可以做任何他们想做的事情。这是大量安全问题的根源，我之前已经写过相关文章。 EmDash 插件在沙盒隔离区中运行，并且必须显式声明它们需要访问的内容，类似于 OAuth 的工作方式。老实说，整个项目最有趣的事情。数据库结构看起来很坚固。我查看了 SQLite 表，我喜欢我所看到的。帖子类型有自己单独的表格，而不是将所有内容都塞进少数通用表格中。如果您曾经调试过 WordPress 数据库，您就会知道那是多么令人耳目一新。这是真正的人工智能优先。有一个 MCP 服务器、一个 JSON CLI 和内置的直接代理访问。Joost 说得很好：
  2. 它不是一个带有 AI 功能的 CMS。这是一个 CMS，其中人工智能代理是一流的构建者。
  3. 这个框架是准确的。 EmDash 将代理视为系统的实际用户，他们可以内省结构化内容和类型化模式。这是一个真正的赌注，从一开始就内置。是否有回报取决于人工智能辅助工作流程的实际落地，但我发现这是 EmDash 最难忽视的部分。 Astro 核心。使用 Astro 作为主题而不是 PHP 模板和functions.php 是一个不错的举措。现代开发人员很熟悉，主题文件中不再散布随机的 PHP 逻辑。也就是说，Astro 模板和 PHP 模板在概念上并没有那么不同。您仍然可以在其中编写混乱、难以维护的代码。真正的胜利是生态系统和围绕它的工具，而不是一些神奇的安全保证。是的，它带回了一些古腾堡之前的怀旧之情。管理面板具有旧的tinyMCE 感觉。主题的工作方式让我想起在全站编辑之前构建 WordPress 主题。模板文件，the_content()，一个简单的循环。我收到上诉了。但我认为完全放弃块编辑器是一个错误。古腾堡确实存在问题，但结构化、可视化内容编辑的想法并不是其中之一。 Matt Cromwell 直截了当地问道：“未来真的是 Astro + TinyMCE 吗？真的吗？”老实说，我认为他说得有道理。为什么我不会使用它 我迁移到 Astro 的部分原因是为了摆脱 CMS。我的博客是 Markdown 文件。没有数据库，没有管理面板，没有可能出错的内容层。我写了一篇文章，推送它，完成了。如果您想了解更多关于内容管理为何对我如此重要的背景信息，我已经写了一些关于内容管理的不同方法的文章。 EmDash 构建得很好，但这意味着运行服务器、支付边缘计算费用以及在我和我的内容之间添加一层。这是我已经认为不值得的权衡。我认为更多的人正在悄然得出同样的结论。 Markdown 文件、静态站点生成器、用于版本控制的 Git。无需 CMS。对于个人博客和小型网站来说，这通常就足够了。它能解决正确的问题吗？我不太确定 EmDash 是否能解决人们对 WordPress 的主要不满。性能、托管成本、插件膨胀。这些才是真正让人们把目光转向别处的东西。 EmDash 并没有真正直接接触这些。它解决的是开发人员体验、安全模型以及人工智能如何融入内容管理。但对 WordPress 感到沮丧的人通常会对缓慢的托管、昂贵的插件或过多的维护开销感到沮丧。 EmDash 并没有让这一切变得更便宜或更简单。对于关心架构的开发人员来说，它变得更加优雅。观众人数比宣传中显示的要少。仍然值得一看 Astro 本身从第一天起就没有取得明显的成功。经过时间、强大的社区和大量的迭代才成为今天的样子。 EmDash 很早，而且有些地方还很粗糙。但它所做的不仅仅是将现有工具包装在更好的用户界面中。我会留意的。我希望它能赢得发展所需的社区。

Original Summary

  1. It’s April 1st. A new CMS drops, built by Cloudflare, powered by Astro, pitched as a modern spiritual successor to WordPress. Sure. Very funny. Except it wasn’t a joke. EmDash is real, and of course I had to take a look. Two posts got me started: Cloudflare’s own announcement and Joost’s take. What is it? EmDash is a fully open source CMS built on TypeScript and Astro 6.0. It runs serverless on Cloudflare Workers (or any Node.js server), uses SQLite locally and Cloudflare D1 in production, and stores content as portable text (structured JSON) instead of raw HTML. The pitch? It’s what WordPress would look like if you built it today, from scratch, without 20 years of legacy decisions weighing it down. Things I actually like The security model is genuinely clever. In WordPress, plugins can do almost anything they want. That’s the root of a huge number of security issues, and I’ve written about this before. EmDash plugins run in sandboxed isolates and have to explicitly declare what they need access to, similar to how OAuth works. Honestly the most interesting thing about the whole project. The DB structure seems solid. I had a look at the SQLite tables and I liked what I saw. Post types get their own separate tables instead of everything being crammed into a handful of generic ones. If you’ve ever debugged a WordPress database, you know how refreshing that is. It’s AI-first in a real way. There’s an MCP server, a JSON CLI, and direct agent access baked in. Joost put it well:
  2. It’s not a CMS with AI features bolted on. It’s a CMS where AI agents are first-class builders.
  3. That framing is accurate. EmDash treats agents as actual users of the system, with structured content and typed schemas they can introspect. It’s a real bet, built in from the start. Whether it pays off depends on where AI-assisted workflows actually land, but it’s the part of EmDash I find hardest to dismiss. The Astro core. Using Astro for themes instead of PHP templates and functions.php is a nice move. Familiar to modern developers and no more random PHP logic sprinkled through your theme files. That said, Astro templates and PHP templates aren’t that different conceptually. You can still write messy, hard-to-maintain code in either. The real win is the ecosystem and tooling around it, not some magical safety guarantee. And yes, it brings back some pre-Gutenberg nostalgia. The admin panel has that old tinyMCE feel. The way themes work reminds me of building WordPress themes before Full Site Editing. Template files, the_content(), a straightforward loop. I get the appeal. But I think dropping a block editor entirely is a mistake. Gutenberg has real problems, but the idea of structured, visual content editing isn’t one of them. Matt Cromwell asked it straight: “Is the future really Astro + TinyMCE? Really?” And honestly, I think he has a point. Why I won’t use it I migrated to Astro partly to get away from the CMS. My blog is markdown files. No database, no admin panel, no content layer that can go wrong. I write a post, push it, done. I’ve written a bit about different approaches to content management if you want more context on why this matters to me. EmDash is well built, but it means running a server, paying for edge compute, and adding a layer between me and my content. That’s the trade-off I already decided wasn’t worth it. And I think more people are quietly arriving at the same conclusion. Markdown files, a static site generator, Git for version control. No CMS required. For personal blogs and small sites, it’s often just enough. Does it solve the right problem? I’m not totally sure EmDash addresses the main frustrations people have with WordPress. Performance, hosting costs, plugin bloat. Those are the things that actually make people look elsewhere. EmDash doesn’t really touch those directly. What it does tackle is the developer experience, the security model, and how AI fits into content management. But the person frustrated with WordPress is usually frustrated about slow hosting, expensive plugins, or too much maintenance overhead. EmDash doesn’t make that cheaper or simpler. It makes it more elegant for developers who care about architecture. That’s a smaller audience than the pitch suggests. Still worth watching Astro itself wasn’t an obvious success from day one. It took time, a strong community, and a lot of iteration to become what it is today. EmDash is early and rough in places. But it’s doing something other than just wrapping existing tools in a nicer UI. I’ll keep an eye on it. And I’m hoping it earns the community it needs to grow.

评论（默认中文）

ramon156 2026/04/03 00:54
这篇文章生成得非常糟糕，除了“我让一个法学硕士深入研究了代码，这是它选择用什么词来描述 EmDash”之外，没有任何真正的收获。

> 乔斯特说得好：

> 这不是一个带有 AI 功能的 CMS。这是一个 CMS，其中人工智能代理是一流的构建者。

Joost 问 ChatGPT 关于 CMS 他应该说什么，你觉得这是一个很好的引用。

> 为什么我不会使用它

> 我迁移到 Astro 的部分原因是为了摆脱 CMS。

那么您从一开始就不需要 CMS 吗？我也不需要为我的网站使用 CMS，但我仍然为客户维护 CMS，因为他们确实需要它。

> 它能解决正确的问题吗？

这是我这篇文章中唯一关心的，答案是【词袋】。人们真的如此渴望将自己的名字命名在新技术上吗？是不是“我想加入！”是什么让人们在生命中度过一个小时？

> Astro 本身从第一天起就没有取得明显的成功。

Astro只是他们构建的框架，这句话和EmDash有什么关系呢？我对这篇文章试图告诉我的内容感到非常困惑。

另外，你怎么没有写一下WordPress刚刚发布时的情况呢？我确信有足够多的人可以提供帮助。它有竞争对手吗？我不知道，出来的时候我正在吃沙子。

Embarrassingly badly generated article, with no real takeaway other than "I let an LLM dig into the code, here's what words it chose to describe EmDash".

> Joost put it well:

> It’s not a CMS with AI features bolted on. It’s a CMS where AI agents are first-class builders.

Joost asked ChatGPT what he should say about the CMS, and you felt like it was a good quote.

> Why I won’t use it

> I migrated to Astro partly to get away from the CMS.

Well then you never needed a CMS in the first place? I also don't need use a CMS for my site, but I still maintain a CMS for customers because they do need it.

> Does it solve the right problem?

This is the only thing I cared about from this article, and the answer is [bag of words]. Are people really this desperate to put their names on new tech? Is it an "I want to be included!" mindset that gets people to prompt an hour of their life away?

> Astro itself wasn’t an obvious success from day one.

Astro is just the framework they built on, what does this sentence have to do with EmDash? I'm so confused about what this article is trying to tell me.

Also, how come you did not write anything about what it was like when WordPress had just released? I'm sure there are enough people who can help out with that. Did it have competitors? I wouldn't know, I was eating sand when it came out.

在 HN 中查看
steve_adams_86 2026/04/03 01:24
说 Astro 从第一天起就没有成功，这是不言而喻的。没有哪个 JavaScript 框架从第一天起就取得了明显的成功。他们怎么可能呢？即使是设计精良、创新的框架和库也很难在如此拥挤的空间中获得采用，因为像框架一样重要的工具具有很大的惯性。确实是一堆话。
To say Astro wasn't a success from day one is a truism. No JavaScript frameworks have been an obvious success from day one. How could they be? Even very well-designed and innovative frameworks and libraries struggle to gain adoption in such a crowded space where tooling as significant as a framework has major inertia. It really is a bunch of words.

leetrout 2026/04/03 01:05
它现在位于此链接下方的首页上，但仅供稍后查看的任何人使用：

EmDash – WordPress 的精神继承者，解决插件安全问题

https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=47602832

It's on the front page right now below this link but just for anyone looking later:

EmDash – A spiritual successor to WordPress that solves plugin security

https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=47602832

在 HN 中查看
nine_k 2026/04/03 03:18
作者指出，他们的网站是静态生成的结果，而 EmDash 需要 HTTP 服务器和一堆静态文件旁边的服务器。

这在一定程度上是公平的。任何不动态变化的东西 每次重新加载 应该生成一个静态文件，并按原样提供。当通过管理界面更改支持数据时， 然后 该页面应该重新生成。我认为这是正确的缓存方法，并且我认为任何理智的 CMS 现在都会这样做（是的，甚至是 Wordpress）。

但静态网站无法提供特定于用户的内容。 EmDash 具有复杂的用户访问级别结构、各种身份验证方法等。一旦您必须拥有用户会话，就无法使用应用程序来处理此问题。最简单的例子是有一个评论部分。

The author notes that their website is a result of static generation, while EmDash needs a server beside an HTTP server and a bunch of static files.

It's partly a fair point. Anything that does not dynamically change with each reload should be generated a a static file, and served as such. When the backing data change via the admin interface, then the page should re-generate. I suppose it's the proper caching approach, and I assume any sane CMS does this now (yes, even Wordpress).

But what a static site cannot do is user-specific content. And EmDash has a elaborate structure of user access levels, various auth methods, etc. Once you have to have a user session, there's no way around having an application that handles this. The simplest example is having a comments section.

在 HN 中查看
Chaosvex 2026/04/03 11:46
您可以使用页面片段来足够接近。在它成为实际功能之前，我在 Varnish 中将其与后端处理的自定义标头一起破解。有点痛苦，但非常可行，即使在几年前
You can use page fragments to get close enough. I hacked this together in Varnish with custom headers handled by the backend before it was an actual feature. A bit of a pain but eminently doable, even years ago
在 HN 中查看
givan 2026/04/03 01:04
我正在开发 Vvveb CMS，WordPress 非常鼓舞人心，是业内每个人都比较的标准。

WordPress 如此普遍的原因之一是它非常容易托管并且不需要高级的技术知识。

PHP 托管非常便宜，WordPress 安装也非常简单，只需在某些托管仪表板中单击一下即可。

Javascript 生态系统很复杂，您需要成为一名开发人员并有权访问命令行来安装大多数 Javascript CMS，并且需要 vps 或更昂贵的托管。

I'm developing Vvveb CMS and WordPress is very inspiring, the standard to which everyone compares in the industry.

One of the reason WordPress is so ubiquitous is that it's very easy to host and it doesn't need advanced technical knowledge.

PHP hosting is very cheap and WordPress installation is very easy, it's just one click in some hosting dashboards.

The Javascript ecosystem is complex, you need to be a developer and have access to command line to install most Javascript CMSes and need a vps or more expensive hosting.

在 HN 中查看
cloudpeaklabs 2026/04/03 01:28
托管故事是 WordPress 最大的护城河，大多数 JS CMS 项目都低估了它。尽管我认为差距正在缩小 - 带有 PM2 的 5 美元 VPS 上的 Node 应用程序正在接近共享 PHP 托管的简单性，并且像 Railway 和 Fly 这样的平台已经使部署几乎变得轻松。真正剩下的障碍是非技术用户无法像通过 cPanel 管理 WordPress 那样自行管理 Node 进程。
The hosting story is WordPress's biggest moat and most JS CMS projects underestimate it. Though I'd argue the gap is narrowing - a Node app on a $5 VPS with PM2 is getting close to shared PHP hosting simplicity, and platforms like Railway and Fly have made deploys nearly painless. The real remaining barrier is that non-technical users can't self-manage a Node process the way they manage WordPress through cPanel.

在 HN 中查看
patates 2026/04/03 01:37
人们还低估了来自法学硕士的index.php 的新力量。

告诉 claude 为你的作品集 html 模板创建一个 php 后端，将生成的文件拖到最便宜的服务器上，这样你就已经有了一个自定义的 CMS。

What people also underestimate is the new power of the index.php that comes from the LLMs.

Tell claude to create a php backend to your portfolio html template, drag the generated file to the cheapest server, and you already have a custom CMS.

Neither the "Cloudflare’s own announcement" nor the "Joost’s take" links point to the right URL, should be lower case.

Thanks. I realized I was writing "emdash" and when I fixed it, I fixed a bit too much.

Emdash has nothing in common worh wordpress. There are no blocks

RobotToaster 2026/04/03 03:51
任何挑战 wordpress 的事情都是积极的。

也就是说，除非他们获得一个良好的免费插件生态系统，否则我认为它不会走得太远。

Anything that challenges wordpress is positive.

That said, unless they get a good ecosystem of free plugins I don't see it going far.
